headline: White House AI crackdown opens door for Chinese model makers
detail: US policy tightening around AI could create uncertainty for domestic AI-infrastructure names - chips, cloud, and cybersecurity all feel this. Your entire book is concentrated here - CRWD, SMH, SOXL, QQQ are all exposed to any risk-off rotation if AI capex narratives get questioned.
rec: No trade yet - but if follow-through policy details hit this week, SOXL and SMH are your most vulnerable positions given leverage and margin. Watch SMH $600 level as an early warning line.

headline: Small-cap stocks enjoy best first half in 35 years
detail: Strong small-cap performance signals broad risk appetite, which is a positive backdrop for your tech and semi names. It also means rotation money could shift away from large-cap tech if the trend continues.
rec: No action needed directly - it's a tailwind for sentiment today, but if rotation accelerates out of mega-cap tech into small-caps, QQQ and SMH could lag. Just something to monitor, not a reason to trim now.

headline: Trump Iran strikes fit a surprising 2026 pattern
detail: Geopolitical escalation in the Middle East historically spikes oil and creates short-term risk-off moves. For your tech-heavy, margin-amplified book, any broad market selloff hits harder than average.
rec: No specific trade - but geopolitical flare-ups are exactly when leveraged positions like SOXL get hit first and hardest. Make sure your stops are set and not just mental notes.

headline: A 23% plunge in personal wealth leaves one country trailing peers
detail: Context-dependent macro signal - suggests global wealth contraction in at least one major economy, which can weigh on global tech demand and chip export outlooks. Relevant given SMH's exposure to international semiconductor revenue.
rec: Too vague and geographically unclear to trade against directly - no action warranted, but it's one more reason to stay disciplined on SMH and SOXL stop levels rather than adding exposure.
